documents.title: Features
documents.content: ## OpenAI-Compatible API UnifyRoute provides drop-in compatible endpoints with OpenAI's API. Use it with any existing SDKs, tools, or applications without modifications. ### Supported Endpoints - **POST /api/v1/chat/completions** - Chat completion requests - **POST /api/v1/completions** - Text completion requests - **GET /api/v1/models** - List available models ## Tier-Based Intelligent Routing Route requests intelligently across multiple providers with automatic failover and redundancy. - **Pri

documents.title: Api Reference
documents.content: ## Overview UnifyRoute provides OpenAI-compatible API endpoints that work with any OpenAI SDK or tool without modifications. **Base URL**: `http://localhost:6565/api/v1` **Authentication**: Include your API token in the Authorization header: ```bash curl -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_TOKEN" \ http://localhost:6565/api/v1/chat/completions ``` ## Chat Completions Create a chat completion for a given prompt. **Endpoint**: `POST /api/v1/chat/completions` ### Request ```bash curl -X POST http://

documents.title: Architecture
documents.content: ## System Overview UnifyRoute is built as a microservices-oriented architecture with the following components: ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Client Applications / SDKs │ └────────────────┬────────────────────────────────┘ │ OpenAI-Compatible API │ ┌────────▼────────────────────┐ │ API Gateway (FastAPI) │ │ - Request validation │ │ - Authentication │ │ - Rate limiting │ └────────┬────────────────────┘ │ ┌────────▼──────────────────────────┐ │ Router Component │ │ - Int

documents.title: Cli
documents.content: ## Overview UnifyRoute provides a comprehensive CLI for managing the gateway, providers, tokens, and configuration. **Usage**: `./unifyroute [COMMAND] [OPTIONS]` ## Global Options ```bash --help, -h Show help message --version, -v Show version information --config, -c PATH Path to config file (default: config.yaml) --debug Enable debug output ``` ## Setup & Services ### setup Initialize UnifyRoute with interactive configuration wizard. ```bash ./unifyroute setup [OPTIONS] ``` **Options**: - `--s

documents.title: Configuration
documents.content: ## Configuration Files UnifyRoute uses configuration files in YAML format. The main configuration file is typically `config.yaml`. ### Configuration Hierarchy 1. `.env` file - Environment variables 2. `config.yaml` - Main configuration 3. Environment variable overrides ## Environment Variables ### Core Settings ```env # Server SERVER_HOST=localhost SERVER_PORT=6565 SERVER_WORKERS=4 DEBUG=false # Security MASTER_PASSWORD=your-secure-password JWT_SECRET=your-jwt-secret-key JWT_ALGORITHM=HS256 JWT_

documents.title: Deployment
documents.content: ## Docker Deployment ### Using Docker Compose Create `docker-compose.yml`: ```yaml version: '3.8' services: redis: image: redis:7-alpine ports: - "6379:6379" volumes: - redis_data:/data healthcheck: test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"] interval: 5s timeout: 3s retries: 5 unifyroute: build: . ports: - "6565:6565" environment: - SERVER_HOST=0.0.0.0 - SERVER_PORT=6565 - REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379 - DATABASE_URL=sqlite:///./unifyroute.db - JWT_SECRET=${JWT_SECRET} - MASTER_PASSWORD=${MASTER_PASSWORD
documents.excerpt: ## Docker Deployment ### Using Docker Compose Create `docker-compose.yml`: ```yaml version: '3.8' services: redis: image: redis:7-alpine ports: - "6379:6379" volumes: - redis_data:/data healthcheck: t...

documents.title: Getting Started
documents.content: ## Prerequisites Before installing UnifyRoute, ensure you have: - **Python 3.11+** - Core language requirement - **Node.js 18+** - Required for the frontend dashboard - **npm** - Node package manager - **Redis** - For session and queue management (optional for development) - **uv** - Fast Python package installer ## Installation ### 1. Clone the Repository ```bash git clone https://github.com/unifyroute/UnifyRoute.git cd UnifyRoute ``` ### 2. Configure Environment Copy the sample environment fil

documents.title: Troubleshooting
documents.content: ## Common Issues & Solutions ### Installation & Setup #### Python Version Error **Error**: `Python 3.11+ required` **Solution**: ```bash # Check Python version python --version python3.11 --version # On Ubuntu/Debian sudo apt-get install python3.11 python3.11-venv # On macOS with Homebrew brew install python@3.11 # Set alias if needed alias python=python3.11 # Or use virtual environment python3.11 -m venv venv source venv/bin/activate ``` #### Redis Connection Error **Error**: `Cannot connect to
